title: DDL, Constraints, Schema Evolution, and Integrity Repair · Data Contract Brief: Migration Expand Contract
summary: The support schema must reject impossible states and evolve while old application versions are still active. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql migration expand contract increment in a constraint-rich, reversible schema migration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plan backward-compatible expand, backfill, verify, switch, and contract phases with rollback evidence.
Choose restrict, cascade, set-null, or set-default actions from lifecycle ownership semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Migration Expand Contract · Theory Migration Expand Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — Import ticket 202 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Migration Expand Contract contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Migration Expand Contract · Theory Migration Expand Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for plan backward-compatible expand, backfill, verify, switch, and contract phases with rollback evidence. For Predict Migration Expand Contract · Theory Migration Expand Contract, keep Migration Expand Contract connected to executable relational evidence.
